entry.title: Re: 🔴 Filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in AEM
entry.content: Can we automatically filter on the key fields of the rap bdef where the event is declared?

entry.title: Re: T093B - Asset Accounting table issue
entry.content:
Hi

check in transaction OAOB if company code is assigned to chart of depreciation. You may also try to delete the depreciation area and create again and assign it to company code.

kind regards

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Hello Samuel,

I'm not totally sure what happens in your system, but I have a suspicion: Did you maintain the “Independent Summarization of Entry View (BSEG)" for the AWTYP 'FKKSU'? FKKSU are the documents transferred from FI-CA to FI-GL. And these are never BSEG summarized, regardless of any OBCY customizing! (Please see note 336454 ). I know it's a bit sad, but for historical reasons FI-CA still reconciles their totals records against BSEG, so BSEG summarization is prevented (hard-coded) for the FI-CA transfer postings to GL. We don't have a 999 limitation for these postings (since FI-CA splits the transfer postings into smaller batches), but we do have the data volume issue. AND: BSEG summarization is also a precondition for ACDOCA summarization, i.e. you cannot summarize more in ACDOCA than in BSEG. (Technically, the ACDOCA-BUZEI is not cleared for ACDOCA summarization). So it's possible that your ACDOCA CO-PA characteristics are cleared (according to your configuration), but eventually the items are not summarized due to the missing BSEG summarization (BSEG-BUZEI).

What doesn't really fit to this story is the fact that you tell me BSEG-PAOBJNR is cleared. I'd have expected that for FKKSU postings, no BSEG summarization, but also no clearing of the maintained fields should happen. You might want to recheck in your system.

You might also want to create an incident for FI-CA for suggestions how to limit the data volume during transfer to GL.

Kind regards, Stefan

entry.title: Re: How to display action result data in Page using rules...
entry.content:
Hi @panushjain,

We are setting value in 'testValue' in ClientData but values are not getting displayed. Please check the attached screenshots.

Thanks and Regards,

Madhav

entry.title: Re: SSAM MDK Xcode distribution to App Store Connect fail...
entry.content: Based on this: https://me.sap.com/notes/3352943, I had to downgrade XCode to 15.4, now it works as before

entry.title: how to convert numbers to dimension member info in sac using scripting?
entry.content:
Hello,

I have manually inputed "202309" in my input field and I have a dropdown with numbers 1 to 6 in it. The initial requirement was to add the dropdown numbers to the input field value. Lets say if I choose 2 in dropdown the input field will show 202311 as a result. I used the below code and it worked fine.

var before_LC=ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(InputField_9.getValue())+ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(Dropdown_2.getSelectedKey());
ScriptVariable_1 = before_LC.toString();

 

Now the requirment is that I need that input field to read that value (202311) as a date dimension ID so that later I can have a date dimension member description for the value (which would be Nov 2023).

Do we have any script for that?

Any support would be appreciated.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Used case scenario of usage of AI in project functions
entry.content:
Hi All,

Have anybody used Gen AI in SAP any of the functions for SAP PS in their recent projects and can give some ideas in where they can be used potentially.

You can also give some links to any articles where SAP mentions any use cases.

 

Regards,

Suman Misra

entry.title: Re: Asset Write-up (ABZU) for Prior Fiscal year in Prior...
entry.content: SAP does not allow write-ups to be posted in Prior Periods. Only Unplanned Dep. We had to post the write-up for prior years in the current year!

entry.title: Re: No PMR - Production material request EWM created
entry.content: WM request indicator is 2 in the profile and assigned to the material.

entry.title: Re: Getting a copy of SAP Certificate
entry.content:
Hi @paoladanaia 

Thank you for bringing this to our attention.
In order to provide individual support please create a ticket in our User Support Center

https://lso-ssrv-app.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/

To help the support team to understand your query better, could you please provide the following information in your correspondence?

  •            Detailed description of the issue
  •            Screenshots: To help us visualize the issue
  •            Please also provide the Course Code of course.

Kind regards,
Margit

entry.title: Re: Exclude inactive condition from subtotal
entry.content: Be careful with exclusions, contiions inactive are used to calculate the subtotal if the condition should be print "a" in pricing procedure (only if customer delivered is in brazil)

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA - BADI (read-only Fields Properties) not found in service header
entry.content:
Hi all,
  In s4 Hana cloud key user Extensibility  in screen  'Create Customer Project' or 'Plan Customer Project'  we have created custom fields ' Currency Value' and 'Exchange Rate' are should be only make it has Read Only, this is requirement we got currently,  we have used 'Adapt UI' , 'Custom fields and logic'   and ' Badi'  and there is no Badi for read only, the Badi is available only for validations and creation of screen Create customer project of custom field, so there is no option to find for the custom field make it read only.
please if any knows this requirement tell me the procedure how do we make it field read only below the comment with screen shots, we are big thankful for who are helping on this requirement, its urgent requirement please expert person tell me the solution as soon as possible. 
thank you.

entry.title: Re: Changes In Funds Management with S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi Frank, thanks a lot for your reponse. You said this is changed for On-Prem also. But I believe that until the 2023 on-prem version, we still had Fund Centers and commitment items. In which on-prem version was this changed?

entry.title: PS Assessment Cycles impact on Budget
entry.content:
Hi Fans,

I would like to create assessment cycles to allocate costs from central WBS to regional WBS. However the central WBS's Budget must NOT be released when the costs are assessed to the Regional receiver WBS.

The receiver WBS's budget must also not be impacted (For this I'm thinking to exempt secondary CE from Budget AVAC settings). However for the central receiver, I need to budget to remain consumed.  

Using a settlement rule in the central receiver will ensure the budget remains consumed, but settlement rules are not practical to use where hundreds of WBS are being posted. 

Any ideas  welcome?

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing for Insights: Line Monitor features 2308/2311

Patch 8 will be released this week.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

Let me also express my strong interest in the Digital Skills Initiative!

My name is Iryna Kriuchkova and I am from Ukraine. When the war started my husband joined Ukrainian Armed Forces and I together with our three children moved to Germany. Currently, I am actively learning (German language and AI) and looking for a job.

I hold two degrees: a Bachelor's in Computer science and a Master's in Accounting. I've been working in software development as a Business analyst/Product owner/Project manager. I also have some experience with ERP implementation. This is why I think I may be a good fit for the program and can quickly start bringing value as a SAP Consultant or even a SAP Developer.

I am super interested in Artificial intelligence (recently I joined the AI Fundamentals certification program) so here I would love to learn about all SAP's AI-related initiatives.

I will be happy to join and start this exciting journey!

 

Best regards,

Iryna Kriuchkova

entry.title: Re: Writing Table data to csv or text file in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Julian,

I understood the question like if DSP can write CSV files (probably as part of an automated data flow), not if it is possible to export the data on UI level.
If it is just about accessing data via excel, then ODBC access would also work (i have posted a link above), and you could then save it in whatever format you like.

Best Regards,

Nico

entry.title: Unable to install SAP Cloud Application Studio
entry.content:
I have a MAC, but I use Windows through Azure AVD. I should be able to install SAP Cloud Application Studio (SDK) and I have already downloaded it on my machine. The SDK requires Visual Studio version 15, which I also downloaded to my machine. I also downloaded the necessary extension 3, which is also needed to install the SDK. However, when I try to install the SDK, the system claims that I do not have the required extensions, even though the extensions have been downloaded. How do I get out of this?

I have the correct version of the SDK and likewise, as mentioned, I have downloaded the necessary extensions and software.

Can't the SDK be accessed via a virtual machine? Is that the reason? 

Any tips or advices are highly appreciated. 

Thanks in advance, 

BR:

Essi

entry.content: I guess that the function module ADS_SR_OPEN creates a spool request, so you can change the page format by debug for the test.

entry.title: Re: The value of a Solution Standardization Board (SSB) as part of implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud, e
entry.content: Thank you @andremalan , no stress if not able to

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - Receive http request with multipart/forrm-d...
entry.content:
Hi larsgies,

you need to place the Multipart-Decoder between the https sender channel and your Groovy script with the option "Multipart Headers Inline" deselected. Also be sure to list the "content-type" http header in the "Allowed Header(s)" in your IFlow. Then the first part of the multipart message will become the message body and the other parts will be transferred as message attachments after the decoder step.

BR, Benjamin

entry.title: Re: MDG: Dump while creating Change Request
entry.content:
If you see the Error in Accessing File Upload and Download in the path: 

Financial Accounting Governance àMultiple Object processing àFile Upload/Download



Below Resolution steps helped me to solve issue - 3311756 - Setting 0G Data Model as default while opening File Upload in MDG. As mentioned in one of replies, adjust Model from 0F to 0G in USMD2000 - Personalization: Default Data Model.

 

Thanks, 
Anil

entry.title: Send Notification Approval Request to Users if the Vendor Master Changed
entry.content:
Hello 
I have Requirement to Send Mail Notification or Workflow Approval to MY Inbox if the Vendor Master Data Changed for the Sensitive Field that Customized at SPRO > FA > AR and AP > Vendor Accounts > Master Data > Preparations for Creating Vendor Master Data > Define Sensitive Fields for Dual Control (Vendors)
that Need approval through FK08.

entry.title: Re: Badi implementation in fb70
entry.content:
So, you mean that you don't know the type of the variable returned by the method GET_DATA of CL_EDOC_SOURCE, because the type is generic:

  methods GET_DATA
    exporting
      value(ES_DATA) type ANY .

Hopefully you can get the data reference via GET_DATA_REFERENCE:

  methods GET_DATA_REFERENCE
    returning
      value(RD_DATA) type ref to DATA .

Your code will be:

DATA(ld_data) = io_source->get_data_reference( ).
ASSIGN ld_data->* TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_data>).

entry.title: Re: How to create Row level Security on user groups in Da...
entry.content:
If you have S/4, you can create a CDS view using the tables usrbf2 and ust12 to get a list of users who have access on S/4 for certain cost centers.  You can then use this CDS view in Datasphere for your DAC.  

Regards,

Mel Calucin

Please let us know, how to make the provision for creation/add 1 more tab in equipment master.

entry.title: How to do the Encoding Conversion by using Groovy?
entry.content:
Hello  Experts,

I am now trying to implement a script in SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration. (Used to be SAP CPI)

The scenario I am trying to realize is the encoding conversion between Shift_JIS and UTF-8. 

I am thinking that users can just specify "Convert From" and "Convert To", and the script will do the conversion.

 

Here's the code that I wrote. However, when I did the experiment, for some reason my original file was deleted and there's no new file generate.

Could someone kindly help with how I can write the script?

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets
import java.nio.charset.Charset

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // User-editable variables
    def convertFrom = "UTF-8"  // Set this to either "UTF-8" or "Shift_JIS"
    def convertTo = "Shift_JIS"  // Set this to either "UTF-8" or "Shift_JIS"

    println("Starting charset conversion from ${convertFrom} to ${convertTo}")

    // Validate and normalize charset names
    convertFrom = normalizeCharsetName(convertFrom)
    convertTo = normalizeCharsetName(convertTo)
    
    // Get the body as a byte array to preserve the original encoding
    def bodyBytes = message.getBody(byte[])
    println("Original body size: ${bodyBytes.length} bytes")
    
    // Perform the conversion
    def convertedBytes = convertCharset(bodyBytes, convertFrom, convertTo)
    println("Converted body size: ${convertedBytes.length} bytes")
    
    // Set the converted byte array as the new body
    message.setBody(convertedBytes)
    
    // Set the output charset
    message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", convertTo)
    
    println("Conversion complete. Headers: ${message.getHeaders()}")
    
    return message
}

def String normalizeCharsetName(String charset) {
    switch (charset.toLowerCase()) {
        case "utf-8": return "UTF-8"
        case "utf8": return "UTF-8"
        case "shift-jis": return "Shift_JIS"
        case "shift_jis": return "Shift_JIS"
        case "shiftjis": return "Shift_JIS"
        case "sjis": return "Shift_JIS"
        default: 
            println("ERROR: Unsupported charset: ${charset}")
            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unsupported charset: " + charset)
    }
}

def byte[] convertCharset(byte[] input, String fromCharset, String toCharset) {
    if (fromCharset == toCharset) {
        println("No conversion needed, charsets are the same")
        return input
    }
    
    try {
        Charset sourceCharset = Charset.forName(fromCharset)
        Charset targetCharset = Charset.forName(toCharset)
        
        String decodedString = new String(input, sourceCharset)
        byte[] result = decodedString.getBytes(targetCharset)
        
        println("Conversion successful: ${fromCharset} to ${toCharset}")
        return result
    } catch (Exception e) {
        println("ERROR: Error during conversion: ${e.message}")
        throw new RuntimeException("Error during charset conversion", e)
    }
}

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to hide categories with no products?
entry.content:
I used this and it worked well

.filter(category -> !productService.getProductsForCategory(category).isEmpty())

entry.title: How to rename a hostname (network name) in a SAP cluster group?
entry.content:
This blog explains how to rename a cluster network name resource in a Windows Failover cluster running SAP NetWeaver or S/4 based applications. The rename of a “virtual” hostname used by SAP applications (that’s SAPLOCALHOST and SAPGLOBALHOST hostnames) is a manual procedure which is described here in detail for the File-Share-Cluster (FSC) and the Shared-Disk-Cluster (SDC) variants.

PowerShell lovers

This blog uses the GUI based tool Failover Cluster Manager to show what needs to be changed. I refer to this blog for the related PowerShell commands: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-sap-cluster-group-from-scratch-with-powershell/ba-p/13897651

 

There are two scenarios when a rename of a cluster network name resource is required:

  1. The SAPLOCALHOST or SAPGLOBALHOST hostnames must be changed in an already existing, productive clustered SAP system.
  2. You created a “clone” of a productive clustered SAP system and want to switch during a short downtime from the productive used system to the “clone”.

 

Prerequisites

Regardless which scenario is used, before you change anything in a productive SAP system make sure you have a current file system backup, which includes the so called “System State”. This contains the cluster configuration as well.

 

   

For scenario 2 you have to create a “clone” in advance. The terminology “clone” refers in this blog to a freshly, new created SAP system in a new cluster. The new system in the new cluster uses the same SAP SID, it uses the same Windows domain users and domain group. But it cannot use the same hostname for the ASCS instance (SDC) and eventually the same hostname for SAPGLOBALHOST (-> FSC).

The productive system still uses the hostname “asc-ABC-prod”. The new system in the new cluster uses a different hostname, in this example it’s “ascs-ABC-clone”. The new system can be used, tested and verified if it works properly, before it will be exchanged with the older, productive system.

Important remark!

Make sure you have the proper authorizations to change the related computer objects in your Active Directory (AD)! (see Appendix)
When it comes to the planned downtime to stop production and to delete the computer object(s) of the network name(s) in AD and you don't have the proper authorizations to do so, then you cannot continue.
Test this in advance!

 

Migration / Rename

SAP system is fully stopped, all instances are offline. The following steps describe scenario 1, the rename of a network name used by SAP applications in an existing cluster. Scenario 2 is equal, but you have to remove the network name in the productive cluster first, before you can add it to the second cluster. Then the procedure is the same as in scenario 1. 

This is where we start:

SAP system with all instances is stopped. SAP ASCS instance is stopped in Failover Cluster Manager tool, related service is stopped, IP, network name, File Server resource are stopped. Only the shared disk will be online.

We cannot simply remove/delete the network name resource, because another resource depend on it, it's the File Server resource.

Remove the dependency in the File Server resource:

Press the Delete button. Only the disk is left.

Delete the network name resource, also called by Microsoft "Client Access Point" (network name and IP address):

You can now create a new DNS hostname with the same IP address or another IP address.

In this example I created a new DNS entry:   IP: 10.17.244.36, hostname:  sap-ascs-test

Select the SAP cluster group, right mouse-click on "Add Resource -> Client Access Point":

Result:

Add the new dependency to File Server cluster resource:

Bring the File Server resource online:

You cannot start the SAP resources at this point.

We must now adapt the configuration to use the new hostname "sap-ascs-test".

Rename the current profile of SAP ASCS instance.
Old:

New:

Edit all profiles and change SAPLOCALHOST and SAPGLOBALHOST accordingly, depending on you configuration (SDC or FSC). In this example we have a SDC configuration, therefore SAPLOCALHOST and SAPGLOBALHOST must be changed to the new hostname. Look for other profile parameter which use the old hostname and change them accordingly.

In my example I have to change

in ASCS instance profile:

SAPGLOBALHOST = sap-ascs-test
SAPLOCALHOST = sap-ascs-test
_PF = $(DIR_PROFILE)\ABC_ASCS00_sap-ascs-test
wdisp/system_0 = SID=ABC, MSHOST=sap-ascs-test, MSPORT=8100, SSL_ENCRYPT=2

changes in ERS profiles:

SAPGLOBALHOST = sap-ascs-test
SCSHOST = sap-ascs-test

changes in default profile:

SAPGLOBALHOST = sap-ascs-test
rdisp/mshost = sap-ascs-test
enque/serverhost = sap-ascs-test

If you miss any entries in the profiles, the related SAP instance will not start or work properly!

SAP services changes. You must to this on all cluster nodes and on all additional application server instances, standalone gateway or Web Dispatcher instances, too. Open Windows Registry editor, navigate to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services and look for the SAP services. Change the "ImagePath" and change the profile path according to the new hostname. Do this for all SAP services of all SAP instances on all Windows hosts of this SAP system!

Result:

Log on with <SID>adm user to the Windows host and change the user environment of <SID>adm user to reflect the new hostname.

Example

Change every environment variable which contains the old hostname.

In my ABCadm user environment this was:

RSEC_SSFS_DATAPATH=\\sap-ascs-test\sapmnt\ABC\SYS\global\security\rsecssfs\data
RSEC_SSFS_KEYPATH=\\sap-ascs-test\sapmnt\ABC\SYS\global\security\rsecssfs\key
SAPEXE=\\sap-ascs-test\sapmnt\ABC\SYS\exe\uc\NTAMD64
SAPLOCALHOST=sap-ascs-test
PATH variable (!!!)

Depending on your configuration, you can have additional environment variables set.
Check also Windows system environment if somebody configured additonal environment variables (which contain the old hostname) there.

Bring online the SAP resources in Failover Cluster Manager:

Congratulations! You have successfully renamed the hostname of a clustered SAP ASCS instance!

 

Appendix

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit - looking for customers/partners projects (field mapping)
entry.content:
Hello all Migration Cockpit users,

We are thrilled to announce a new customer initiative that we have launched on October 15th. We are currently seeking participants to collaborate with us on an AI use case/idea. We are specifically looking for customer and partner projects that are at the starting point of a migration project coming from non-SAP legacy systems to S4/HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For more information and to register, please visit Customer Engagement Initiative.  We are looking forward to welcoming numerous participants and working together in exploring the potential of AI in data migration. 

Best regards,
Sybille Lackermeier, SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit Product Management

Kind regards
karin

entry.title: Rise into the future with a virtual event on October 22
entry.content:
Join SAP leaders, customers, partners, and industry experts virtually to learn how business AI, cloud ERP, and a connected supply chain can transform your business.

Join the Rise into the Future event to:

  • Be the first to see the new embedded AI capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Supply Chain, enabled by Joule
  • Discover new streamlined and AI-assisted ABAP and SAP Build capabilities
  • Catch the latest updates from RISE with SAP Methodology including the generative AI-enabled assistant
  • Hear from customers who have successfully transformed their businesses with RISE with SAP and are embracing AI

To get a head start, jump over to the SAP Learning site and dive into our latest future-focused learning journeys on the focus topics: “Exploring SAP Cloud ERP | SAP Learning”, “Applying the RISE with SAP Methodology”, “Provisioning and Implementing Joule”, and “Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition”.

Register now

entry.title: Accelerate AI-Powered Insights with Joule
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced, data-driven world, staying ahead requires more than just access to data - it demands the ability to harness that data effectively. Enter Joule, a game-changing AI solution designed to deliver faster, smarter, and more impactful decision-making. With Joule, organizations can seamlessly integrate AI into their existing SAP systems, transforming raw data into actionable insights that drive innovation and efficiency.

Getting Started with SAP Joule
But how do you get started with SAP Joule? For those looking to elevate their SAP skills and implement AI-powered solutions, the journey begins with the right training. Before diving into a full Joule implementation, it's crucial to build a solid foundation.

Build Your Foundation: Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio
The Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio course is a free, self-paced learning journey that introduces core AI concepts within the SAP ecosystem. This course provides a comprehensive understanding of how SAP leverages AI, preparing you to maximize the value of Joule and other AI solutions.

Master Joule: Provisioning and Implementing Joule
Once you've laid the groundwork, the next step is the Provisioning and Implementing Joule learning journey. Designed for IT professionals and SAP users alike, this course equips you with the knowledge and skills to effectively provision, deploy, and implement Joule. By completing this journey, you'll not only learn how to unlock the full potential of Joule in driving analytics and operational efficiency but also earn a Record of Achievement, showcasing your understanding of Joule’s capabilities.

 Incorporating Joule into your organization is a powerful way to enhance decision-making, streamline processes, and stay competitive in an AI-powered future. Ready to take the next step? Start your learning journey today and transform how your organization approaches insights and efficiency with SAP Joule.                          

Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Re: SAP Recommendation: Assignment of Corporate Accounting Principle to Ledger
entry.content:
Hi,

In my project, 5WE scope item is activated for group valuation. So, Can you please suggest how I can assign accounting principles to ledger 0L,2L,3L and 4G?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello

First of all, I want to thank you for this program, as it encourages people to learn more about SAP certification, including myself.

Let me briefly introduce myself. I just completed the MM course on SAP's web learning platform, and thanks to that, I passed the certification exam and obtained the MM certificate. While studying MM, I explored the EWM field related to extended warehouse management and found it very interesting. Therefore, I want to further study EWM and take the EWM certification exam. After completing the web course, I will send you an email and look forward to receiving the attempt from you soon.

entry.title: Re: Modify mail text in work item
entry.content:
Hi. 

Sorry for the delay in answering. 

I have been able to fill the text in the task. Now my problem is: the mail is been sent four or five days after we created the purchasing order but we don't know how this mail is sent. 

Do you know anywhere in customizing or in the workflow where we could custom an automatic mail?

Thanks a lot.

Marta.

entry.title: Re: Fundamentals of Security in SAP BTP
entry.content: It is very comprehensive, I would like to know if it is possible to create a Client Credential for a technical service to manage sub-accounts under the given global account.

entry.title: Re: PaPm cloud Count number employees or other field
entry.content:
Hi @juancortes,

I would suggest you to use a window aggregate function to count the employee numbers in a (model) calculation (in universal model). According to your requirement following window function might be used:

COUNT(DISTINCT EMPLOYEE) OVER (PARTITION BY AREA) 

COUNT(DISTINCT EMPLOYEE) OVER (PARTITION BY YEAR-MONTH)

I hope this would be helpful.

Best regards,

Zhiyi

entry.title: Re: How do we create a variable on WEBI that calculates i...
entry.content: the formula "Couple" is not available in webi, could your please provide another formula

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@MartinRaepple Great guide, and looking forward for the upcoming ones. Especially looking for the "deeper" integration which also populates SAP Cloud Identity's identity directory, in line with our strategic direction (see CIO guide).

A detail: I see you only add OIDC scope "offline_access" to the trust between IAS and Entra. I suggest adding the standard OIDC scopes "profile" and "email", to make sure BTP gets the typical user attributes in the standard token claims.

entry.title: Unable to populate data in sales order AVC using power automate & scripting
entry.content:

The Sales order item is AVC configurable.

The nitial GUI Screen data entry done well. When I go for AVC Screen data entry It dont work. 

Power Automate Error - Failed to write in textbox (Textbox wasn't found)

Text to fill in - %CurrentItem['CHARECTERISTIC']%

entry.title: Re: CDS Data Model for Analytical List Page
entry.content:
@RadekP 

For an ALP, a normal CDS View with measure fields annotated with Aggregation.default would be sufficient, and there is no need to create an analytical query.

entry.title: Multiple Ledgers in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear All,

We will define multiple ledgers these are 0L(IFRS) and 2L(J-GAAP) according to SAP recommend in Public Cloud.
In this regard, I have two questions.

①There are no plans that My customer will use IFRS yet.
In the future, they will use IFRS(Abroad expansion).
In this case, is it not necessary that My customer process 0L about closing operation?
If there is task, let me know about that.

②
If they will use multiple local account standard (multiple countries), must they define such local account standard in 2L every conpany code?
※ At the time of introduction, they only need to activate 0L and 2L?

Best Regards,

Yuki Tsumura

entry.title: Implement Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Objective

Currency Conversion is an important aspect of any data modelling, planning, and reporting. SAP Datasphere software provides a standard functionality for Currency conversion. However, this is a quick step by step process on how currency conversion can be implemented with federation using S/4 HANA as a source. This article is completely based on my learning experience. I hope, this article will be useful for those consultants who are looking to capitalize the virtualization, federation, and tailor the needs based of the custom requirement.

How is it  different from standard approach?

1. This approach does not need all the currency relevant tables for the basic currency conversion but have the minimum four tables that are primarily involved as mentioned in the pre-requisites

2. Tables are federated or remote or virtual physical present in S/4 HANA. Unless there is a need for persistency can be scheduled at the input layer

3. Tables when remotely configured can be named according the naming conventions of the business need purposes

Prerequisites

  • Connection of SAP S/4 HANA on Premise or Cloud systems
  • Below standard tables activated in S/4 HANA systems

Table Name

Table Description

TCURV

 Exchange rate types

TCURX

 Decimal places in currencies

TCURR

 Exchange rates

TCURF

 Conversion factors

Option 1:

 Code snippet for SQL view

SELECT "FISCALYEARPERIOD", "PRODUCT","COMPANYCODE", "TRANSACTIONCURRENCY","AMOUNTINTRANSACTIONCURRENCY"

 

CONVERT_CURRENCY( AMOUNT => "AMOUNTINTRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

SOURCE_UNIT => "TRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

   "CONVERSION_TYPE" => 'M',

    "REFERENCE_DATE" => '2024-09-01', ----can be an input parameter

    "CLIENT" => '400',

    "SCHEMA" => 'DW_DEV_SCHEMA',

    "ERROR_HANDLING" => 'set_to_null',

    "TARGET_UNIT" => 'SAR', -----can be an input parameter

"PRECISIONS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURX', --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

    "CONFIGURATION_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURV', --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

    "PREFACTORS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURF', --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

    "RATES_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURR' --the table used for conversion must be declared here this is federated with remote table call

/***These remote tables when imported have been named with prefix as S4H in this case.

)

AS deb_cre_lc,

 

from "FIGL_LINEITEM" ----ACDOCA standard SAP Transaction Table

Resultset:

When converted from USD to SAR

Option 2:

Case of calculated column in a graphical view

Calculated column dbe_cre_lc definition

Code snippet

CONVERT_CURRENCY( AMOUNT => "AMOUNTINTRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

SOURCE_UNIT => "TRANSACTIONCURRENCY",

   "CONVERSION_TYPE" => 'M',

    "REFERENCE_DATE" => :IP_REFERENCE_DATE,

    "CLIENT" => '400',

    "SCHEMA" => ‘DW_DEV_SCHEMA',

    "ERROR_HANDLING" => 'set_to_null',

    "TARGET_UNIT" => :IP_TARGET_CURRENCY,

"PRECISIONS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURX',---federated tables from S/4 HANA

"CONFIGURATION_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURV', ,---federated tables from S/4 HANA

"PREFACTORS_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURF', ,---federated tables from S/4 HANA

"RATES_TABLE" => 'S4H_TCURR') ,---federated tables from S/4 HANA

INPUT PARAMETERS

Resultset:

But apparently ,it dosenot any effection in monthly pay Halfday absence unpaid , because in pay roll in schema :

question : can I do any customising for handel it or should I chnage schema?

entry.title: SYSTEM user locked
entry.content:
Hi,

I reset the password of system user of systemdb, but when i tried to login it fails... i see the below messages in the nameserver trace file...

Please help to unlock the SYSTEM user of SYSTEMDB

178818]{-1}[27/4944874] 2024-10-16 13:19:28.852833 e Authentication catalog_authmgr.cc(00902) : lock time for user SYSTEM is 1440 minutes; user is locked until 2024-10-17 08:14:04.7510000 (given in UTC) [1440,2024-10-17 08:14:04.7510000]

entry.title: Re: #CoE#HHT How to add printer to SAP Cloud Print Manage...
entry.content:
Hello @FFreya  Thanks for your question, It's great to see that you are utilizing SAP Cloud Print Manager. Here are the steps to address your questions:

1. How to add a new local printer to SAP Cloud Print Manager: SAP Cloud Print Manager automatically detects printers that are configured on your local machine. Ensure the printer you want to add is correctly installed and set up on your computer. Here’s how you can typically add a new local printer on your Windows machine, which will then be available in SAP Cloud Print Manager: - Navigate to Settings > Devices > Printers & scanners. - Click "Add a printer or scanner". Windows will search for available printers. - Choose the printer you want to add from the list and follow the on-screen instructions to install it. - Once installed, restart SAP Cloud Print Manager, and the new printer should appear in the list of available printers. If the printer is not detected automatically by SAP Cloud Print Manager after these steps, make sure that the printer is shared in your network (if applicable) and that it’s set as a default printer, as sometimes this can affect visibility.

2. Where to find the printed documents if you choose the printer Microsoft Print to PDF: When you select "Microsoft Print to PDF" as your printer in SAP Cloud Print Manager, it essentially lets you save the output as a PDF file instead of printing it out on paper. Here’s how you can locate or specify where these PDF files are saved: - After selecting "Microsoft Print to PDF" as the printer and initiating the print command, a save dialog box should typically appear asking you where to save the PDF file. - In the dialog box, you can select the desired location (folder/directory) on your computer where you'd like to save the PDF file. You can also rename the file in this step before saving. If, for some reason, you are not seeing this save dialog box, check the printer settings for "Microsoft Print to PDF" in your computer’s printer settings (accessible via **Settings > Devices > Printers & scanners**). Sometimes, there's a default save path set, and you might need to change it to prompt for a location each time.

I hope this resolves your issues. Let me know if you have any more questions!

Best regards

Dave

entry.title: Re: How do we create a variable on WEBI that calculates i...
entry.content:
There not couple function because is not what i wrote. Could you first give us a simplified sample of your dataset ?
ex :

client numbernb dayslab 1lab2
xxx150structure 1structure 2
xxx230structure 2structure 2
xxx30structure 1-
xxx4100structure 1structure 4
...   

Is it something like that ?
So here it will be number([client number]) where ([nbdays]<=60)
or number([client number]) where ([nbdays]<=60 and [lab2]<>"-")

entry.title: Re: Introducing new type of Data Access Control "Operator and Value" for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Great article. 

Just a quick note. If using the CP or LIKE operator(s) then you need to wrap the value in the first value column in %'s. 
So if I'm looking for say Regions like 'West' my DAC table is looking for:
Criterion: Region

Operator: LIKE

First Value: %West%

There wasn't an example of this up above.

entry.title: Re: Error occured in number assignment for object RESB
entry.content:
Error occured in number assignment for object RESB

Message no. CO168

Diagnosis

An internal error has occurred.

System Response

Processing stopped.

Procedure

Contact your system administrator.

entry.title: Field ISDfCSTEx in ADM1: where is it managed?
entry.content:
Hello guys,
I've had a problem try to saving a "Company details" update having the blocking message below:
"Value not valid; value points to invalid or nonexistent object".

Investigating, I found the field ADM1.ISDfCSTEx, that is related to the ODCI table, which had a value present in the linked table but not compliant to the Country of the company and that causing this saving block.

Please, could someone tell me in which GUI/Interface I should find this field?
I've to understand where the field is managed, but I looked into Company details, general settings and document settings and I didn't find it.

Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: Error in Parser-Thread - CPARSER
entry.content: Well for interest, as you will notice, it is now 2024...13 years after this post started and today I am still got this error and searching brought me here 🙂

entry.title: Re: Can I connect DEV and PRD DMC instances to one PCo?
entry.content:
Hi,

At this point in time, you must consider migrating to SAP Production Connector asap, see https://me.sap.com/notes/3513927 for more details.

Regarding connecting a single SAP Production Connector to multiple SAP Digital Manufacturing instances: No, this is not supported. See: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/production-connectivity-model/add-production-connector-sap-plant-connectivity-systems, which mentions:

 
Note
A Production Connector can be connected to a single SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant only. Further connections from any other SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant to the same Production Connector are rejected.

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: why users without access to invoices have access to D...
entry.content:
Hi, @janickovicpeter.

Have you checked the General Authorizations?

Document Draft auth is located within the Sales A/R bracket, even so, it is supposed to serve all marketing documents. So you can set it up like the below and review the other brackets such as Purchasing - A/P.


BR,
Felipe

entry.content:

Ja zatem proponuje 15 listopad i 20 grudnia. Pozdrowienia!

entry.title: Enabling More Secure and Responsible Generative AI: Key Insights from SAP Teched
entry.content:
The recent SAP TechEd session, presented by @BettinaLaugwitz and Franziska Dobrigkeit, shed light on how SAP is leading the way in ensuring the security and responsibility of generative AI. In an era where AI technologies are becoming omnipresent, SAP is embedding robust ethical guidelines and governance frameworks into its AI processes. 

The Pillars of Responsible AI

Their approach to responsible AI is anchored in three key pillars: AI Safety and Ethics, AI Security, and AI Compliance.

1. AI Safety and Ethics

At the heart of responsible AI lies a commitment to ethical standards and fair outcomes. This involves ensuring that AI systems are designed to promote inclusivity and mitigate biases that can lead to discrimination. As generative AI often learns from uncurated datasets, there’s a pressing need to actively counteract the encoding of harmful patterns within AI models.

SAP’s approach to AI ethics revolves around four key requirements: human agency and oversight, fairness and nondiscrimination, transparency and explainability, and privacy and data governance. The AI Safety and Ethics requirements mandate safeguarding human autonomy through a human-in/on-the-loop approach, ensuring AI systems do not perpetuate marginalization or discrimination, prioritizing transparency and explainability in AI decisions, and protecting privacy while maintaining data integrity. These principles help create AI systems that are trustworthy and equitable.

2. AI Security

As businesses increasingly rely on cloud-based solutions, SAP emphasizes the importance of robust AI security measures. This includes implementing security controls specifically tailored for SAP’s cloud solutions, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected from threats.

Security is paramount in SAP’s AI framework, particularly for cloud solutions. SAP ensures the security of their cloud solutions from SAP and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) by implementing rigorous AI security controls. These controls protect the data and processes pivotal to AI model training, ensuring that AI solutions are not only effective but also secure from external threats and vulnerabilities.

3. AI Compliance

The evolving landscape of AI regulations, such as the EU AI Act and the US Executive Order 14110, requires organizations to stay ahead of compliance requirements. SAP’s commitment to regulatory adherence ensures that its AI initiatives not only meet legal standards but also align with ethical norms. Compliance with legal and regulatory standards is the third pillar of SAP’s responsible AI strategy. SAP applies stringent checks through audits and regulatory compliance processes to ensure their AI models and data usage meet international standards. They manage data for model training with utmost scrutiny, ensuring compliance with regional and industry-specific regulations.

AI Safety and Ethics

The foundation of SAP’s responsible AI strategy lies in their comprehensive safety and ethical guidelines. SAP’s commitment to AI safety and ethics is divided into internal governance and operationalization on one hand, and adherence to external influences and standards on the other. Internally, the SAP Executive Board, supported by the SAP AI Global Ethics Steering Committee and the AI Ethics Office, drives ethical governance. They rely on guiding principles such as the UNESCO recommendation on the ethics of AI, ensuring these are reflected in product standards and a trustworthy AI workstream.

Externally, SAP aligns with crucial ethical standards and recommendations from organizations like UNESCO, the OECD, IEEE, and more. They adhere to responsible AI regulations like the EU AI Act and the US Executive Order 14110 and take guidance from the SAP AI Global Ethics Advisory Panel. This dual approach ensures that SAP’s AI innovations are ethically sound and globally compliant.

The AI Ethics Assessment Process

SAP’s AI ethics assessment process delineates how AI use cases are evaluated for potential risks and compliance. This process, as depicted in the flowchart image above, involves impact assessments, categorization of cases as redline, high-risk, or standard, and subsequent steps to ensure adherence to SAP's AI ethics policies.

Implementing Responsible AI

SAP has established a comprehensive assessment process to evaluate AI initiatives against key ethical criteria:

  • Human Oversight: Ensuring human agency in AI decision-making processes to maintain autonomy.
  • Fairness and Non-Discrimination: Actively working to prevent biases from being perpetuated in AI outputs.
  • Transparency and Explainability: Striving for clarity in how AI systems are developed and how they operate, even when dealing with complex models.
  • Privacy and Data Governance: Maintaining strict protocols to protect individual privacy and the integrity of data.

Innovative Solutions for Ethical AI

To operationalize these ethical principles, SAP has introduced several innovative solutions:

Orchestration: By designing streamlined AI workflows that connect various components, SAP enables organizations to focus on innovation without getting bogged down in integration challenges. This ensures that AI workflows are powerful yet manageable, connecting diverse components like data pipelines and AI models, as illustrated in the orchestration pipeline flowchart. Orchestration minimizes maintenance while maximizing the efficiency of AI deployment.

  • Grounding: Enhancing the contextual relevance of AI outputs by enriching requests with pertinent business data, reducing the likelihood of inaccurate or misleading responses. This ensures that AI workflows are powerful yet manageable, connecting diverse components like data pipelines and AI models. Orchestration minimizes maintenance while maximizing the efficiency of AI deployment.

  • Data Masking: Protecting sensitive information while retaining the necessary context for AI applications, thereby mitigating biases and safeguarding user privacy. A crucial measure helping to avoid bias and privacy breaches.

  • Content Filtering: Implementing filters to screen out harmful or biased outputs, ensuring that generative AI remains a safe and trustworthy tool for users. This module allows filtering undesired prompts and responses, thereby avoiding biased, harmful, or unethical AI outputs.

As the landscape of AI continues to evolve, fostering responsible innovation will be essential to build trust and ensure a positive impact on society.

SAP’s approach to responsible and secure generative AI, guided by key principles and stringent frameworks, demonstrates a robust model for ethical AI deployment. By combining internal governance with adherence to external standards, SAP not only pioneers in AI innovation but also ensures these advancements are ethical, secure, and compliant. The insights from the SAP TechEd session offer a comprehensive understanding of how SAP addresses the critical challenges of AI ethics and governance, providing a blueprint for the future of responsible AI.

entry.title: Re: Changes In Funds Management with S/4 Public Cloud
entry.content: No, there is no change in OnPremise/Private Cloud.

entry.title: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content:
Hello Gurus!!

Greetings !!

Having a problem. Using BDC record i need to complete PGI against Delivery order.

Problem is its skipping one line every time to updated data. I can not find out the problem.Sample code is given here as reference. Please help me to sort it out. Also item number not perfectly maintain which is given in the attachment even its take an extra line also.Its an urgent issue. Please help me.


  LOOP AT it_lips INTO wa_lips.

    indx = indx + 1.

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '1000'.

    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=POPO_T'.

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '0111'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'RV50A-POSNR'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=WEIT'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'RV50A-POSNR'
                              wa_lips-posnr.


***WHEN ON Below code ONE LINE SKIP  & SCROLL WORK PERFECTLY WITH UPDATED VALUES
****WHEN OFF ITS SCROLL WORKS PERFECTLY BUT DATA NOT UPDATED

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '1000'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                              '/00'.


    DATA(txt_lg) = |LIPS-LGORT({ indx })|.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING txt_lg
                                  '0004'.

    DATA(txt_dlqt) = |LIPSD-G_LFIMG({ indx })|.
    txt_qt = wa_lips-lfimg.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING txt_dlqt
                                  txt_qt.


    DATA(txt_pk) = |LIPSD-PIKMG({ indx })|.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING txt_pk
                                  txt_qt.
  ENDLOOP.


  PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV50A' '1000'.
  PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                '=WABU_T'.
  PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                'LIPSD-PIKMG(01)'.

  PERFORM bdc_transaction USING 'VL02N' 'A'.

entry.title: Re: It is possible to have a Sandbox Server on SAP RISE for 6-9 months?
entry.content: Hi Paul, any feedback yet or work around? We also need a sandbox server in SAP RISE to test if our industry specific variant configurator is still working and if linked production software running in another cloud subscription is still working fine (no latency issues).

entry.title: Re: SYSTEM user locked
entry.content:
Please look into these KBA from SAP:

  • Reset the SYSTEM User Password in the System Database | SAP Help Portal
  • 2681997 - You have reached maximum number of invalid password entry attempts. User is locked until ... - SAP for Me
  • 2736167 - How to unlock/reactivate the SYSTEM user on SAP HANA? - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: BDC Page scroll Issue
entry.content: Could you please embed the screenshots instead of hyperlinks? Note that Batch Input (BDC has been obsolete for 20 years) doesn't permit to scroll, instead you must use any function code which permits to reposition (scroll) the table control (it depends on the transaction code and screen). At last resort, you may extend the standard to permit this feature and use it via Batch Input.

entry.title: Content Server 7.53/4 Monitoring in the Solution Manager 7.2
entry.content:
Hello, 

I already know about the following note, describing the approach on how to connect a Content Server for Monitoring:
https://me.sap.com/notes/1895715

it has an attached PDF that acts as Manual. However some informations are missing in my opinion.

If i look at the process for the CS 6.5, it describes how you can attach a Database to the System, however this is not the case with the 7.53/4 CS. It seems that i am not able to add a Database to the System, so that both are linked together. Of couse the Database can be monitored independently, however it is quiet a hassle. As you have no direct link to the System and the Database if you look things up in the LMDB or you have to plan out the System and the database seperatly in the it Calendar. 

I wonder if there is another way of attaching a CS to a Solution Managaer?

Also is it possible to not add them as a system Type "Web Dispatcher" is this is super confusing?

 

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Service PO price update based on Credit Note
entry.content:
Dear SAP Colleagues,

Please share your experience in handling below scenario in purchasing:

Scenario: Service PO > SES complete & posted > Invoice posted & paid. Now vendor sends a CN, how we can get the PO line item amount updated bringing back the amount equal to CN?

Thanks 

Atif
